title: cyanus tchihatcheffii
intro: Cyanus tchihatcheffii, also known as Tchihatchew's cornflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant's flowers and roots are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cyanus tchihatcheffii include its potential to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its flowers and roots in water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
